reasoning: The 10x threshold for this question translates to approximately 194 'Riot' events in a single 30-day period (based on the reference average of ~19.3). This represents an extraordinary level of civil unrest, comparable only to black-swan events like Chile's 2019 'Estallido Social.' Recent news indicates no ongoing uprisings of this magnitude in Chile during July or early August 2025, keeping the lowest possible bounds for the near-term resolution dates (August 10 and September 2, 2025) at the baseline minimum (0.05 to 0.06). While October brings the anniversary of the 2019 protests, and November 2025 will hold the general elections (with potential December runoffs), recent reporting states the political climate in Chile has pivoted much more toward security concerns rather than revolutionary reform, meaning an unexpected massive spike of 200+ riots in a month is still highly unlikely. I increase my probabilities slightly for November and January (0.08 and 0.11) to account for general uncertainty at a longer time horizon and the remote possibility of election-related unrest, but my overall forecasts remain deeply anchored to the base rate of typical stability in Chile relative to this extraordinarily high required threshold.
